title: Maerua siamensis
taxonomy: Maerua siamensis belongs to the family Capparaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the caper family. This family includes about 200 species of plants found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Maerua siamensis is a species of flowering plant in the genus Maerua, which is a genus of flowering plants native to Africa and Asia. The genus Maerua is often included in the family Capparaceae, although some sources may place it in the family Brassicaceae. The classification of Maerua siamensis can vary depending on the source and the current understanding of plant taxonomy.
uses: Maerua siamensis is reportedly used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. Its extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
